methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainbow Robotics, a public-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oh Jun-ho with $50M+ raised to date, operates within a fragmented peer universe comprising one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable to this analysis. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains eleven same-sector peers overall, but none satisfy the filtering criteria for a sufficiently narrow comparable set—zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers meeting the minimum threshold of three datapoints. A sector-wide median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics subsegments, deployment models, and commercialization stages represented across the peer universe.

The analysis cannot proceed to triangulation without meeting minimum peer homogeneity requirements. A rigorous valuation would require either: (1) expansion of the peer set to include broader manufacturing automation comparables with documented comparable metrics; (2) direct fundamental analysis if financial and operational data on Rainbow Robotics become available; or (3) recalibration of stage/tier filters to capture a minimum of three methodologically sound comparables. Without quantified revenue, EBITDA, or growth metrics for the target or peer set, no supportable valuation range can be derived from the available data.
